name: 维恩塔 Veyn'tar
prompt:
A towering alien megastructure-organism on exoplanet Detavis. This is NOT a tree — it is a 50-meter tall photosynthetic tower built from stacked, spiraling membrane discs. Each disc is 6 meters wide, deep purple-black, covered in retinal-based pigment molecules. The central column is 4 meters thick, dark violet, covered in geometric fractal patterns of nutrient channels.

The tower stands in a vast plain of identical towers stretching to the horizon, connected underground by visible bioluminescent mycelial networks glowing faintly violet. The ground is dark mineral soil with geometric crystalline formations.

The sky is amber-orange, illuminated by a distant orange dwarf star. Volumetric light shafts pierce between the towers. Billions of dark purple spores drift like alien snow. The scale is immense — the towers dwarf any Earth structure.

Wide-angle landscape shot showing the alien geometry of the tower-plain. Extraterrestrial xenobiology documentation, hyper-realistic, cinematic lighting from an orange dwarf star, amber-hued alien atmosphere, 2.2 atm pressure world. Professional exoplanet field photography.

name: 暗浮藻 Nyxophyta
prompt:
An alien ocean surface that appears as a vast, undulating expanse of LIQUID BLACK INK. This is caused by trillions of microscopic retinal-pigmented organisms (10-50μm) that absorb all visible light, making the ocean surface appear as a void of absolute darkness.

Shot from just below the surface looking up: the water above is pitch black, but shafts of amber light from an orange dwarf star pierce through, illuminating millions of tiny black-purple sparkles — each sparkle is a single Nyxophyta cell catching the light.

Below, in the eternal darkness, strange bioluminescent xenofauna drift past: translucent geometric shapes, pulsing blue-green light patterns, organisms with impossible Earth-like symmetry. The contrast between the black ink ocean and amber light shafts is surreal and alien.

Underwater photography, volumetric lighting, alien seascape. Extraterrestrial xenobiology documentation, hyper-realistic, cinematic lighting from an orange dwarf star, amber-hued alien atmosphere, 2.2 atm pressure world. Professional exoplanet field photography.

name: 琥珀礁虫 Ambercoral
prompt:
A massive alien bio-mineral structure rising from a dark ocean floor. This is NOT coral — it is a colony of millimeter-scale organisms that secrete calcium-amber acid polymer, creating intricate geometric lattice structures in warm amber, gold, and deep orange hues.

The structure spans hundreds of meters, forming impossible architectures: hyperbolic paraboloid shells, fractal branching networks, tessellated honeycomb chambers, and spiraling towers that glow from within. Each surface is covered in tiny amber polyps pulsing with bioluminescence.

The surrounding ocean is pitch black (from dense Nyxophyta above), but amber light filters down, making the entire structure glow like a submerged alien city. Strange multi-limbed xenofauna swim through the lattice openings.

Wide-angle underwater shot showing the alien architectural geometry. Extraterrestrial xenobiology documentation, hyper-realistic, cinematic lighting from an orange dwarf star, amber-hued alien atmosphere, 2.2 atm pressure world. Professional exoplanet field photography.

name: 渊影兽 Abyss Stalker
prompt:
An alien apex predator on exoplanet Detavis. Six articulated limbs arranged in radial symmetry around a central torpedo-shaped body. The body is 3 meters long, covered in tessellated dark purple-gray scales with fractal micro-patterns that absorb light.

The head is a flattened wedge with three large compound eyes (not vertebrate eyes — faceted, multifaceted, glowing purple) arranged in a triangular cluster, plus thermal-sensing pit organs. The jaw splits into four mandibles revealing inward-curving teeth.

The creature crouches in the shadow of a massive purple tower-organism root system, its six limbs positioned for explosive launch. Acidic marking fluid drips from specialized glands, etching patterns into the mineral ground.

Amber light from an orange dwarf star filters through the tower-canopy above, creating dramatic god-rays that illuminate dust and spores. The ground is dark mineral soil with bioluminescent lichen patterns.

Low-angle shot emphasizing the alien radial anatomy and predatory tension. Extraterrestrial xenobiology documentation, hyper-realistic, cinematic lighting from an orange dwarf star, amber-hued alien atmosphere, 2.2 atm pressure world. Professional exoplanet field photography.

name: 深渊噬光者 Void Maw
prompt:
An alien deep-ocean apex predator in the lightless depths of Detavis. The creature is 15 meters long, its body a perfect light-absorbing void — skin covered in nanostructures that absorb 99.9% of all light, making it appear as a hole in reality.

The body is streamlined but with impossible anatomy: no visible eyes, instead covered in electroreceptor arrays (raised hexagonal nodes). The mouth is a circular opening lined with concentric rings of backward-facing barbs, capable of expanding to swallow prey larger than itself. A powerful caudal fin propels it silently.

The background is absolute darkness punctuated by alien bioluminescence: geometric light patterns of deep-sea xenofauna, pulsing blue-green networks, drifting luminous particles. The Void Maw appears as a massive dark silhouette that ECLIPSES the bioluminescence — wherever it moves, light disappears.

Deep-sea photography, extreme low-light, the creature defined by what light it BLOCKS rather than reflects. Extraterrestrial xenobiology documentation, hyper-realistic, cinematic lighting from an orange dwarf star, amber-hued alien atmosphere, 2.2 atm pressure world. Professional exoplanet field photography.

name: 光背行兽 Sunback Grazer
prompt:
An alien mega-herbivore on exoplanet Detavis. Eight articulated limbs arranged in two rows of four, supporting a barrel-shaped body covered in tough, segmented dark gray-violet hide. The creature is 800kg, 1.5 meters at shoulder.

Its most alien feature: the broad dorsal surface hosts a living symbiotic colony of dark purple-black photosynthetic microorganisms (Nyxophyta relatives). This living carpet glistens with an oily purple-black sheen, actively converting amber starlight into chemical energy. Thin translucent nutrient channels connect the microbial layer to the host's circulatory system, visible as glowing purple veins.

The creature walks slowly through a clearing between alien tower-organisms, its eight limbs moving in a wave-like coordination pattern. Small alien organisms ride on its back, tending the photosynthetic garden.

Amber light from above illuminates the purple-black symbiotic layer, which appears to glow with captured energy. Ground-level perspective. Extraterrestrial xenobiology documentation, hyper-realistic, cinematic lighting from an orange dwarf star, amber-hued alien atmosphere, 2.2 atm pressure world. Professional exoplanet field photography.

name: 骨灰菌 Ashbone Recycler
prompt:
An alien decomposer organism on exoplanet Detavis. NOT a fungus — it is a colonial mineral-extractor that grows in overlapping shelf-like plates made of biomineralized calcium-phosphate ceramic, colored bone-white to ash-gray.

Multiple ceramic shelves (10-30cm each) grow from a decomposing alien carcass. The shelves show visible growth rings and mineral crystallization patterns on their edges. The surface has a matte, bone-like texture with embedded mineral particles sparkling.

Scattered around the colony base are hundreds of "bone pearls" — perfect spheres 1-3mm in diameter, each a different pure mineral: white calcium pearls, rust-red iron pearls, yellow phosphorus pearls, black manganese pearls. They gleam like alien jewels against the dark violet forest floor debris.

The decomposing substrate shows enzymatic dissolution in progress — organic matter breaking down while mineral components are extracted and concentrated into pearls. Amber side-lighting reveals the ceramic texture and pearl colors.

Extreme macro photography, shallow depth of field. Extraterrestrial xenobiology documentation, hyper-realistic, cinematic lighting from an orange dwarf star, amber-hued alien atmosphere, 2.2 atm pressure world. Professional exoplanet field photography.

name: 风暴浮母 Storm Drifter
prompt:
An alien atmospheric organism floating in the mid-troposphere of exoplanet Detavis at 8km altitude. The creature is a translucent gas-bladder 3-5 meters in diameter, filled with hydrogen produced by internal symbiotic microbes. The bladder is amber-pink, semi-transparent, revealing internal chamber structures and nutrient channels.

Long trailing tentacles (10-15 meters) hang below, filtering airborne microorganisms from wind currents. The tentacles are semi-transparent with visible internal transport channels glowing faintly blue-green.

The background is spectacular and ALIEN: a permanent mega-cyclone called "Eternal Eye" dominates the sky — towering amber cloud-walls spiraling around a calm dark eye 50km across. Dozens of Storm Drifters float at various altitudes, their bladders glowing amber in the cyclone light, resembling alien jellyfish in an atmospheric ocean.

The amber sky has impossible cloud formations, shafts of orange light breaking through. Aerial perspective showing the vast alien atmospheric landscape. Extraterrestrial xenobiology documentation, hyper-realistic, cinematic lighting from an orange dwarf star, amber-hued alien atmosphere, 2.2 atm pressure world. Professional exoplanet field photography.
